metrics.narrative: Daniel Simmonds articulated qualitative pain on the 12-Jun-2026 discovery call: current procurement process is fragmented (Scanmarket for S2C, Halo for ITSM intake, Chrome River or Anydata for invoicing, Ligura for contract redlining, DocuSign for e-signature), resulting in duplicate data entry, no single source of truth, and procurement team spending 40% of their time on process administration rather than strategic sourcing. He stated we are doing a lot of things twice and there is no visibility across the whole spend, with ~£120M indirect spend managed by 14-person procurement team plus 5 buyers and 5 contract lawyers. However, Daniel has not quantified the cost of these inefficiencies (FTE hours wasted per month, cost per duplicate entry, missed savings opportunity, compliance risk exposure). On the 26-Jun demo, Anthony Gray positioned that Zycus typically delivers 40% procurement efficiency gain and 3-4% tail-spend savings, but Daniel did not validate these numbers against Pinsent Masons baseline. The RFI response due 27-Jul-2026 must quantify the business case: translate 40% efficiency into FTE cost saved (e.g., if 14-person team spends 40% on admin = 5.6 FTE wasted, at £60K average = £336K annual waste), quantify tail-spend savings on £120M spend (3% = £3.6M), and calculate ROI payback period. Without quantified metrics, the business case for budget approval above Daniel's undisclosed threshold will be weak.

champion.narrative: No strong champion identified. Daniel Simmonds (Head of Procurement, Lead Category Specialist for Business and Customer Services) is the primary contact and is cooperative, but he has not demonstrated the hallmarks of a champion: internal advocacy for Zycus (no evidence he is selling Zycus internally vs other RFI vendors), access to and influence over the Economic Buyer (he stated COO is aware but not engaged, and finance lead is unnamed), or ability to navigate internal politics and drive the decision (he stated twice that process beyond RFI is very much up in the air and budget approval is unclear). Daniel is an engaged evaluator and process owner (he scheduled the discovery call and demo, invited Samangy to the demo, issued the RFI with Zycus included), but his engagement is transactional (running a formal RFI process with 7 vendors, not advocating for one vendor). On the 26-Jun demo, Daniel asked tough questions about integration complexity (Adderant, Ligura, Halo), pricing transparency, and customization cost, and he refused to share budget when Claire asked, which signals he is protecting Pinsent Masons interests rather than coaching Zycus on how to win. No other potential champion has emerged: Samangy (data analyst) attended the 26-Jun demo but is not a decision-maker or influencer, and no finance, IT, legal, or C-level contacts are engaged. Champion development is the highest-priority move post-RFI shortlist: if Zycus makes the shortlist of 3 (post-27-Jul), the tailored demo in Aug-Sep must be used to identify and develop a champion, likely by delivering differentiated value (e.g., Merlin AI solving a specific pain for Daniel or Samangy, contract AI proving superior to Ligura for legal team, ROI business case that helps Daniel sell internally to COO and finance) and by multi-threading to find an internal sponsor (finance lead, IT lead, or COO) who can advocate at the exec level.

competition.narrative: Pinsent Masons is running a competitive 7-vendor RFI (issued 28-Jun-2026, responses due 27-Jul-2026), and Zycus is competing against 6 peer vendors plus the do-nothing option (Scanmarket renewal in Dec-2027). The named competitors from the discovery call and demo are: (1) Scanmarket (Unit4), the incumbent S2C sourcing platform with contract expiring Dec-2027, positioned as the default do-nothing option if no new vendor is selected; (2) Halo, the incumbent ITSM service desk platform used for guided intake, which Daniel asked whether Zycus Merlin intake can integrate with or replace; (3) Ligura, the newly signed (12-month contract, last few months) AI contract redlining platform used firm-wide by fee-earner lawyers and procurement, which Daniel asked whether Zycus contract AI can integrate with or replace; (4) Chrome River, Anydata, and DocuSign, the incumbent invoice processing and e-signature platforms that may be in-scope for P2P replacement. The 6 unnamed RFI peer vendors are likely Coupa, SAP Ariba, GEP, Ivalua, Jaggaer, and possibly Scanmarket itself (if bidding to expand scope beyond S2C). The do-nothing risk is high: Daniel stated on 12-Jun and 26-Jun that budget approval is unclear, process beyond RFI is very much up in the air, and P2P scope is uncertain (finance has not committed), all of which signal that Pinsent Masons may complete the RFI, not shortlist any vendor (or shortlist but not select), and default to Scanmarket renewal in Dec-2027. How Zycus wins: (1) prove Merlin AI intake orchestrates with Halo (not replaces it) to add procurement-specific intelligence on top of Halo service desk, positioning Zycus as the only vendor that integrates rather than rip-and-replace; (2) prove contract AI integrates with Ligura (if Ligura allows API access) or demonstrate Zycus Insta Review is superior (side-by-side comparison on same third-party paper), so legal team sees value in consolidating on Zycus; (3) deliver transparent, modular indicative pricing in RFI response (base platform + modules + integration + implementation + maintenance) that is competitive vs Coupa/Ariba/GEP and clearly lower TCO than maintaining the fragmented stack (quantify cost-of-inaction: duplicate data entry, manual reporting, compliance risk, missed savings); (4) quantify ROI business case (40% efficiency gain = £X FTE cost saved, 3-4% tail-spend savings = £Y on £120M spend, payback in X months) to help Daniel secure budget approval and EB sponsorship; (5) position Dec-2027 Scanmarket expiration as forcing function (if no decision by Q4-2026, locked into fragmented stack for 3-5 years) to create urgency and counter do-nothing risk.

identify_pain.narrative: Daniel Simmonds (Head of Procurement) articulated clear operational pain on the 12-Jun-2026 discovery call: Pinsent Masons procurement process is fragmented across multiple disconnected systems (Scanmarket for S2C sourcing, Halo for ITSM intake, Chrome River or Anydata for invoice processing, Ligura for contract AI redlining, DocuSign for e-signature, Adderant ERP for payments and supplier master data), resulting in duplicate data entry, no single source of truth for spend visibility, and the 14-person procurement team spending 40% of their time on process administration and data reconciliation rather than strategic sourcing and supplier relationship management. Daniel stated we are doing a lot of things twice, there is no visibility across the whole spend (£120M indirect), and the team is constantly re-keying data between systems. He also stated that the fragmented stack makes it difficult to enforce compliance (maverick spend, non-contracted suppliers, tail-spend leakage) and to demonstrate ROI to finance and the COO. On the 26-Jun demo, Samangy (data analyst) confirmed that procurement reporting is manual and time-consuming because data lives in multiple systems, and she has to pull reports from Scanmarket, Halo, Chrome River, and Adderant separately, then reconcile in Excel. The pain owner is Daniel Simmonds (Head of Procurement), who is accountable to the COO for procurement efficiency and to finance for spend visibility and savings. The pain is confirmed and articulated, but not quantified (no FTE hours wasted per month, no cost per duplicate entry, no missed savings opportunity calculated yet).

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics and legal/procurement workflow are not yet defined, as the deal is in RFI stage (vendor selection is ~Q4-2026, contracting would follow in 2027). However, several paper-process signals are visible. First, Pinsent Masons is a law firm, so legal review and contracting will be rigorous and likely led by in-house legal team (Daniel mentioned 5-person contract lawyer team on 12-Jun call). Second, Daniel stated on 26-Jun that the legal team signed a 12-month Ligura contract firm-wide for AI contract redlining in the last few months, indicating that legal procurement decisions can move independently of the central S2P initiative and may require separate sign-off. Third, the Scanmarket (Unit4) contract expires Dec-2027, which is a forcing function: if Pinsent Masons does not select and implement a new S2P platform by Q4-2027, they will be forced to renew Scanmarket and lock in the fragmented stack for another 3-5 years. Fourth, integration scope with the relevant stakeholder (practice-management system for payments and supplier master data) and Halo ITSM (service desk intake) suggests that IT and finance will need to sign off on integration feasibility, API access, and data security before contracting. Fifth, Daniel stated on 12-Jun that InfoSec review and data privacy compliance are standard for any enterprise software procurement at Pinsent Masons. No SI or implementation partner has been mentioned, so Zycus is expected to deliver implementation services directly or name a partner in the RFI response. Mitigation: in the RFI response due 27-Jul, outline implementation approach (phased rollout, timeline, Zycus services vs partner, change management plan), confirm Zycus standard MSA/SOW terms are compatible with law-firm procurement (especially data security, IP ownership, liability caps), and position Dec-2027 Scanmarket expiration as the hard deadline for go-live (working backward, vendor selection must happen by Q4-2026, contracting Q1-2027, implementation Q2-Q4 2027).

economic_buyer.narrative: The Economic Buyer (budget owner with authority to approve capital expenditure above the undisclosed threshold) is not identified or engaged. Daniel Simmonds stated on the 12-Jun discovery call that the COO is aware of the S2P initiative but is not actively engaged, and finance is on board at early stage but the finance lead is unnamed and has not attended any calls. On the 26-Jun demo, Daniel stated that budget approval above the undisclosed threshold will require a business case and internal sign-off, but he refused to disclose the budget quantum when Claire asked (I will not share what our existing budget is with you Claire) and would not name who signs off (CFO, COO, or both). The lack of EB engagement is a critical gap: without a C-level or finance exec sponsor who owns the commercial decision, this RFI may be a research exercise rather than a committed buying process, and the deal risks stalling at business-case stage post-shortlist or defaulting to Scanmarket renewal in Dec-2027. The evidence suggests the EB is either the COO (who Daniel mentioned as aware) or the finance lead (who Daniel said is on board but not engaged), but neither is confirmed. Mitigation: in follow-up to the 27-Jul RFI submission, ask Daniel to introduce Zycus to the COO and finance lead for a tailored ROI demo post-shortlist, framing it as helping him build the internal business case, and deliver a quantified ROI business-case template in the RFI response to make the ask compelling.

decision_process.narrative: The decision process is multi-stage but not fully mapped. Stage 1 (current): RFI issued 28-Jun-2026 to 7 vendors (Zycus plus 6 peers, likely Coupa, Ariba, GEP, Ivalua, Jaggaer, Scanmarket incumbent) with responses due 27-Jul-2026. Daniel stated on the 12-Jun discovery call that after RFI responses are reviewed, Pinsent Masons will shortlist 3 vendors for tailored demos in Aug-Sep 2026, then make vendor selection decision, but he stated on both 12-Jun and 26-Jun that the process beyond RFI is very much up in the air, including whether to proceed to formal RFP, whether to make decision based on tailored demos alone, and whether budget approval can be secured. Stage 2 (inferred): shortlist of 3 vendors post-27-Jul, tailored demos Aug-Sep 2026, vendor selection Q4-2026. Stage 3 (inferred): contracting and implementation through 2027 to meet Dec-2027 Scanmarket contract expiration forcing function. The approval chain is partially known: Daniel stated on 26-Jun that budget approval above undisclosed threshold requires business case and internal sign-off, but who signs (CFO, COO, procurement director, or multi-level approval) is not confirmed. Also unknown: who is on the vendor selection committee (procurement, finance, IT, legal, COO), who has veto power, and whether there is a formal scoring rubric or subjective decision. The call sequence shows: 12-Jun discovery call (Daniel solo), 26-Jun demo (Daniel + Samangy data analyst, no finance/IT/legal/exec), RFI issued 28-Jun. The lack of multi-threading and EB engagement suggests this is procurement-led evaluation without exec sponsorship yet, which is appropriate for RFI stage but risky for vendor selection and contracting stages.

sources: Salesforce Next Step 28-Jun-2026: RFI issued to 7 vendors with ~60 requirements, response due 27-Jul
Avoma discovery call 12-Jun-2026, Daniel: requirements include end-to-end S2P, integration with Halo/Adderant/DocuSign/Ligura/Chrome River, ~100 end users, AI capabilities
Avoma demo 26-Jun-2026, Daniel and Samangy questions: Merlin intake integration with Halo, contract AI integration with Ligura, Adderant supplier data integration, modular pricing transparency
decision_criteria.narrative: The RFI issued 28-Jun-2026 contains approximately 60 high-level requirements across S2P modules (guided intake, sourcing, contract management, e-signature, PO/invoice workflows, analytics, AI capabilities), asking vendors to respond can you meet this requirement and is it standard config vs custom development. Daniel stated on the 12-Jun discovery call that the requirements focus on end-to-end S2P with potential P2P extension, integration with existing systems (Halo ITSM, Adderant ERP, DocuSign, Ligura, Chrome River or Anydata), user adoption for ~100 end users, and AI capabilities (intake automation, contract redlining, spend analytics). On the 26-Jun demo, Daniel and Samangy asked detailed questions about Merlin intake (can it integrate with Halo or replace it), contract AI (can it integrate with Ligura or replace it), supplier data management (can it pull supplier master data from Adderant automatically), and pricing transparency (modular cost breakdown: base platform + modules + integration + implementation + maintenance). The decision criteria are partially documented in the RFI requirements spreadsheet (which Zycus will receive 29-Jun), but the relative weighting, must-haves vs nice-to-haves, and evaluation scoring rubric are unknown. Also unknown: whether cost is a knockout criterion, whether AI capabilities are differentiators or table stakes, and whether integration complexity (especially Adderant and Ligura) will disqualify vendors. Mitigation: in the RFI response due 27-Jul, map each of the ~60 requirements to Zycus capabilities with specificity (not generic yes/no), highlight differentiators (Merlin AI, contract AI, orchestration with Halo/Ligura, out-of-box integrations), and address integration feasibility for Adderant and Ligura with proof points or customer examples.

bullets.full: Losing on capability fit — AI fit signal is positive. Daniel Simmonds stated on 12-Jun discovery call that AI capabilities (intake automation, contract redlining, spend analytics, tail-spend visibility) are important evaluation criteria, and on 26-Jun demo he and Samangy showed strong interest in Merlin AI intake and contract AI redlining. However, the strength of AI appetite is not yet clear: Daniel has not stated that AI is a must-have or knockout criterion, and the RFI may not heavily weight AI capabilities vs other factors (pricing, integration, user adoption, implementation timeline). Also, Pinsent Masons legal team just signed a 12-month Ligura contract for AI contract redlining, which suggests they are already investing in AI but may prefer Ligura over Zycus contract AI (or may resist replacing it). The Salesforce AIS fields (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c) are unpopulated, so no quantified AI appetite signal is available from Salesforce. Based on Avoma evidence alone, the signal is AI Curious (interested and asking questions, but not yet committed to AI as primary buying criterion).

cro_panel.header: Pinsent Masons — Formal Evaluation · $200K · closes 31 Dec

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 0: no break-risk was observed in the swept evidence. Coverage is thin, so read this as 'nothing seen yet,' not 'nothing there.'
commentary.win_position: Win 32 is carried by momentum 60 vs stage-expected 52; competitive strength +0.20 (weight 15). Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.40 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 60 (moving forward): Buyer actively engaged this sweep (live pulse + buyer calls). Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, exec and champion, commercial, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment 55 reflects real customer investment: Customer owns 16 open commitment(s); Security/procurement review underway.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 35 is anchored on the stage win (32), nudged by commitment 55 and momentum 60, and dragged by risk 0. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Buyer actively engaged this sweep (live pulse + buyer calls).

ai_fit_signal.summary: AI fit signal is positive. Daniel Simmonds stated on 12-Jun discovery call that AI capabilities (intake automation, contract redlining, spend analytics, tail-spend visibility) are important evaluation criteria, and on 26-Jun demo he and Samangy showed strong interest in Merlin AI intake and contract AI redlining. However, the strength of AI appetite is not yet clear: Daniel has not stated that AI is a must-have or knockout criterion, and the RFI may not heavily weight AI capabilities vs other factors (pricing, integration, user adoption, implementation timeline). Also, Pinsent Masons legal team just signed a 12-month Ligura contract for AI contract redlining, which suggests they are already investing in AI but may prefer Ligura over Zycus contract AI (or may resist replacing it). The Salesforce AIS fields (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c) are unpopulated, so no quantified AI appetite signal is available from Salesforce. Based on Avoma evidence alone, the signal is AI Curious (interested and asking questions, but not yet committed to AI as primary buying criterion).

items.change: Opportunity created, stage Formal Evaluation, amount £200K, close date 31-Dec-2027

Discovery call with Daniel Simmonds: identified fragmented procurement stack (Scanmarket, Halo, Chrome River, Ligura, DocuSign, Adderant), £120M indirect spend, 14-person procurement team, pain of duplicate data entry and no spend visibility, RFI to be issued ~29-Jun to 7 vendors

Art-of-the-possible demo delivered to Daniel Simmonds and Samangy (data analyst): covered S2P/P2P, Merlin intake, contract AI, integrations. Daniel asked about Halo integration, Ligura integration, Adderant supplier data sync, modular pricing. Daniel refused to share budget quantum.
RFI officially issued to 7 vendors (including Zycus) with ~60 requirements, response deadline 27-Jul-2026, per Next Step update

deal_movement.summary: Deal progressed rapidly from creation (12-Jun-2026) through discovery call (same day), art-of-the-possible demo (26-Jun-2026), to RFI issuance (28-Jun-2026). Pinsent Masons is running a formal 7-vendor RFI process with responses due 27-Jul-2026, and Zycus is actively engaged. However, the deal remains single-threaded (Daniel Simmonds only), and Economic Buyer (COO or finance lead) is not engaged. Next milestone: RFI response submission by 27-Jul, then shortlist of 3 vendors for tailored demos in Aug-Sep 2026.

items.detail: Daniel Simmonds expressed uncertainty about budget approval beyond RFI stage on 12-Jun-2026 discovery call, stating whether I can get whether we need additional budget and I can get approval for that is unclear, and whether finance will be included in full P2P scope is up in the air. No EB (CFO, COO, or C-level exec) identified or engaged, and no approved budget quantum disclosed. Risk: RFI may be a research exercise without committed budget or exec sponsorship, leading to do-nothing or delayed decision. Mitigation: map EB and budget approval chain in tailored demo post-RFI shortlist (post-27-Jul-2026), secure EB engagement with quantified ROI business case (40% procurement time saved = X FTE cost, Y% spend-under-management increase, Z savings), and tie urgency to Dec-2027 Scanmarket contract expiration forcing function (if no decision by Q3-2026, default renewal of fragmented stack for 3-5 years). Deliver compelling indicative pricing in RFI response by 27-Jul-2026 to reduce sticker-shock risk and keep Zycus in shortlist.

Integration requirements with existing systems (Halo ITSM, Scanmarket S2C, Adderant ERP, DocuSign, Chrome River) stated by Daniel on 12-Jun-2026 discovery call, with expectation for connectors or orchestration. Anthony Gray stated Zycus has rich library of out-of-box APIs and multi-control-protocol server to integrate with modern systems, but no proof/demonstration yet for Pinsent Masons specific systems (especially Adderant ERP for payments, which is practice-management system not a standard ERP). Risk: if Zycus cannot prove out-of-box or low-effort integration with Adderant, Halo, Chrome River (if P2P in-scope), the single-platform/orchestration value prop is undermined and custom integration cost/timeline may disqualify Zycus. Mitigation: in demo TODAY (26-Jun-2026) or RFI response by 27-Jul-2026, prove (1) Zycus has out-of-box connectors for DocuSign (firm-wide, must work seamlessly) and common ITSM/ERP platforms, (2) Zycus can orchestrate with Halo for intake handoff (Merlin as procurement-specific layer on top of Halo, not replacing it), (3) Adderant ERP integration is feasible (if no out-of-box connector, show API/middleware approach with timeline and cost), (4) Chrome River integration or replacement is clear (confirm P2P scope first). If integration is custom/complex, be transparent on timeline and cost in RFI response to avoid disqualification on hidden-cost grounds.

Single-threaded deal: Daniel Simmonds (Head of Procurement) is sole engaged buyer contact; no IT, finance, legal, or COO stakeholders on discovery call, in contact roles, or confirmed for demo TODAY (26-Jun-2026). Risk: if Daniel leaves, is overruled, or loses internal sponsorship, Zycus has no other relationship in the account. Also, single-threaded deals are vulnerable to ghost/do-nothing outcomes (no internal momentum beyond one person). Mitigation: multi-thread during or after demo TODAY by asking Daniel to invite IT (for integration validation), finance (for P2P scope and budget approval), or COO (for exec sponsorship) to tailored demo post-RFI shortlist (post-27-Jul-2026). Add missing buyer-side contact roles to opportunity after multi-threading. Build relationships with IT and finance before Vendor Selected stage to de-risk single-thread dependency on Daniel.

Do-nothing risk: Daniel expressed uncertainty about process beyond RFI (whether to proceed to RFP or make decision on tailored demos, whether budget approval can be secured, whether finance will be included in P2P scope), stating beyond the RFI it is very much up in the air on 12-Jun-2026 discovery call. Risk: RFI may be exploratory research without committed timeline, budget, or exec mandate, leading to indefinite delay or status-quo continuation (renew Scanmarket in Dec-2027 by default). Mitigation: quantify cost-of-inaction in RFI response by 27-Jul-2026 (40% procurement time wasted = X FTE cost/year, duplicate data entry = Y hours/month wasted, no spend visibility = Z missed savings, fragmented stack = A compliance/audit risk), tie urgency to Dec-2027 Scanmarket contract expiration forcing function (if no new platform go-live by Q4-2027, forced to renew Scanmarket and lock in fragmented stack for another 3-5 years), map EB/COO in tailored demo post-shortlist and secure exec sponsorship with ROI business case, and position Zycus as low-risk choice (92% adoption, 450 customers, proven in legal sector if references available, phased implementation to spread cost and de-risk change mgmt).

Indicative pricing required in RFI response by 27-Jul-2026 per Daniel's 12-Jun-2026 request, but no budget quantum, approval threshold, or cost envelope disclosed by buyer. Risk: Zycus pricing may exceed undisclosed budget or be perceived as high vs peer competitors in RFI shortlist, leading to disqualification before tailored demo. Also, Daniel requested clarity on standard config vs customization cost, so pricing must be transparent and modular (not a black-box enterprise quote). Mitigation: deliver clear, modular indicative pricing in RFI response by 27-Jul-2026 (base platform cost for ~100 end users + module costs for S2C, P2P if in-scope, Merlin, iContract AI redlining, analytics + integration/implementation cost range + annual maintenance), benchmark against typical legal-sector S2P deals of similar size (if available), and position ROI payback (X months to payback based on 40% procurement efficiency gain, Y% spend-under-management increase, Z% tail-spend savings). If pricing is above market or above Daniel's undisclosed budget, Zycus may be shortlisted but not selected, or may not make shortlist at all. Confirm budget expectations during or after demo TODAY if Daniel is willing to share.

Budget quantum and approval threshold undisclosed. Daniel Simmonds stated on 26-Jun that we will need a business case for anything above our existing budget but would not share budget amount when Claire asked. Daniel also stated on 12-Jun and 26-Jun that whether I can get budget approval is unclear and process beyond RFI is very much up in the air. Risk: Zycus indicative pricing in RFI response may exceed undisclosed budget, or EB may not approve business case post-shortlist, leading to do-nothing (Scanmarket renewal Dec-2027). Mitigation: deliver compelling ROI in RFI response by 27-Jul (quantify cost-of-inaction: 40% wasted time = £X FTE cost, Y missed savings, Z compliance risk; quantify payback: X months based on efficiency + spend-under-management + tail-spend savings), map and engage EB (COO, finance lead) in tailored demo post-shortlist to validate ROI and secure budget approval, and position Zycus as low-risk choice (92% adoption, phased implementation to spread cost). If Zycus pricing is above Daniel's undisclosed budget, may not make shortlist of 3.

Adderant ERP integration not yet proven. Daniel stated on 12-Jun that Adderant integration (practice-management system for payments and supplier master data) is expected, and on 26-Jun Samangy asked whether Zycus would pull supplier data automatically. Anthony Gray stated Zycus has APIs to integrate but did not demonstrate Adderant-specific connector (Adderant is not standard ERP, out-of-box connector may not exist). Risk: if Zycus cannot prove low-effort integration with Adderant in RFI response by 27-Jul, single-platform value prop is undermined and custom integration cost/timeline may disqualify Zycus.

Ligura integration or replacement not yet confirmed. Daniel stated on 26-Jun that legal team signed 12-month Ligura contract firm-wide for AI contract redlining (not just procurement, also fee-earner lawyers), and asked is there a way we can still make sure it is part of the overall process (i.e. integrate Zycus with Ligura). Nishita said integration possible if Ligura has open APIs and is not a direct competitor blocking it, but did not confirm. Risk: if Ligura blocks competitor integrations or charges for API access, Zycus contract AI (Insta Review) may not integrate and legal team may resist replacing Ligura (they just signed 12-month contract). This could force Zycus to exclude contract AI module from scope, reducing differentiation and ACV. Mitigation: contact Ligura immediately (before RFI response 27-Jul) to verify API access and confirm they are not blocking competitor integrations. If Ligura allows integration, confirm it in RFI response and prove it in tailored demo. If Ligura blocks integration, position Zycus Insta Review as superior replacement (show side-by-side comparison in tailored demo: Ligura vs Zycus contract AI on same third-party paper) and let legal team decide. If legal team insists on keeping Ligura, Zycus may need to drop contract AI module from scope (reducing ACV and competitive differentiation vs other S2P vendors).

Economic Buyer (COO or finance lead) not identified or engaged. Daniel stated on 12-Jun that COO is aware but not actively engaged, and finance is on board at early stage but finance lead is unnamed. Daniel stated on 26-Jun that budget approval above undisclosed threshold requires business case and sign-off, but who signs is not confirmed. Risk: without EB engagement, deal may stall at business-case stage post-RFI shortlist and default to do-nothing (Scanmarket renewal Dec-2027).

Single-threaded deal: Daniel Simmonds is sole engaged buyer contact (Head of Procurement), Samangy (data analyst) attended demo 26-Jun but is not decision-maker, and no finance, IT, legal, or COO stakeholders in Zycus relationship. Risk: if Daniel leaves, loses internal sponsorship, or is overruled on budget, Zycus has no other relationship. Also, single-threaded deals are vulnerable to ghost/do-nothing when one person loses internal momentum (Daniel stated on 12-Jun that process beyond RFI is very much up in the air, suggesting exploratory mandate not committed mandate). Mitigation: multi-thread in tailored demo post-RFI shortlist (post-27-Jul) by asking Daniel to invite finance lead (for P2P scope and budget approval), IT lead (for integration validation), legal lead (for Ligura decision), and COO if possible (for exec sponsorship). Build direct relationships with each before Vendor Selected stage. Add all engaged buyer stakeholders as OpportunityContactRole contacts (currently only 1 contact, data-quality gap).

P2P scope uncertainty: Daniel stated on 12-Jun and 26-Jun that P2P (invoice approval, Chrome River replacement) is managed by finance and whether that is part of the end solution who knows. Finance lead is on board at early stage but not engaged and has not committed to P2P scope. Risk: if finance does not commit to P2P, deal shrinks to S2C-only (reducing ACV, ROI, and competitive differentiation vs Scanmarket incumbent).

Indicative pricing required in RFI response by 27-Jul-2026, but no budget quantum or cost envelope disclosed by buyer. Daniel requested on 12-Jun that RFI responses include indicative pricing and clarity on standard config vs custom cost, and on 26-Jun he refused to share budget amount when Claire asked (I will not share what our existing budget is with you Claire). Risk: Zycus pricing may exceed undisclosed budget or be perceived as high vs peer competitors (6 other vendors in RFI shortlist, likely Coupa, Ariba, GEP, Ivalua, Jaggaer), leading to disqualification before tailored demo. Also, if Zycus pricing is black-box or lacks transparency (base platform + modules + integration + implementation not broken out), Daniel may perceive it as high-risk. Mitigation: deliver clear, modular, transparent indicative pricing in RFI response by 27-Jul (base platform for ~100 end users + S2C modules + P2P modules if in scope + Merlin intake + iContract AI + analytics + integration cost range for Adderant/Halo/DocuSign/Ligura + implementation cost range + annual maintenance), benchmark against typical legal-sector S2P deals of similar size (if available), position ROI payback (X months to payback based on 40% efficiency gain, 3-4% tail-spend savings, 20% spend-under-management increase), and offer phased implementation to spread cost (Phase 1: S2C + Merlin intake by Dec-2027 Scanmarket expiration, Phase 2: P2P if finance approves, Phase 3: advanced AI features). If Zycus pricing is above market, may not make shortlist of 3.
Budget quantum undisclosed and approval above existing budget requires a business case to an unnamed approver.
No economic buyer engaged; COO only aware, finance on board but unnamed; deal driven solely by procurement.
Adderant ERP (practice-management, non-standard) and Ligura integration not yet proven; could become a cost/complexity disqualifier.
Single-threaded on Daniel; no IT, finance, legal or COO relationship.
Do-nothing/scope-shrink risk: process beyond RFI is 'up in the air' and P2P/finance scope undecided, risking an S2C-only outcome close to the incumbent.

Budget quantum and approval threshold undisclosed. Daniel refused to share budget amount on 26-Jun when Claire asked, and stated on 12-Jun and 26-Jun that budget approval above undisclosed threshold requires business case and internal sign-off. Risk: Zycus indicative pricing in RFI response may exceed undisclosed budget, or Economic Buyer may not approve business case post-shortlist, leading to do-nothing (Scanmarket renewal Dec-2027).

Ligura integration or replacement not confirmed. Daniel stated on 26-Jun that legal team signed 12-month Ligura contract firm-wide for AI contract redlining and asked whether Zycus can integrate with Ligura. Nishita said integration possible if Ligura has open APIs and is not blocking competitor integrations, but did not confirm Ligura API access. Risk: if Ligura blocks integration or charges for API access, Zycus contract AI may not integrate and legal team may resist replacing Ligura (just signed 12-month contract), forcing Zycus to drop contract AI module (reducing ACV and differentiation).

Single-threaded: Daniel Simmonds is sole decision-level contact, no finance/IT/legal/C-level contacts engaged. Risk: if Daniel leaves, loses internal sponsorship, or is overruled on budget, Zycus has no other relationship. Also, single-threaded deals are vulnerable to ghost/do-nothing when one person loses internal momentum (Daniel stated process beyond RFI is very much up in the air).

Indicative pricing required in RFI response by 27-Jul, but no budget quantum or cost envelope disclosed. Daniel requested on 12-Jun that RFI responses include indicative pricing and clarity on standard config vs custom cost, and on 26-Jun he refused to share budget amount. Risk: Zycus pricing may exceed undisclosed budget or be perceived as high vs peer RFI competitors (Coupa, Ariba, GEP, Ivalua, Jaggaer), leading to disqualification before tailored demo.

Do-nothing risk: Daniel stated on 12-Jun and 26-Jun that budget approval is unclear, process beyond RFI is very much up in the air, and P2P scope is uncertain. Risk: RFI may be exploratory research without committed timeline, budget, or exec mandate, leading to indefinite delay or Scanmarket renewal Dec-2027 by default (locking in fragmented stack for 3-5 years).

champion_strength.source: Salesforce OpportunityContactRole (Evaluator), Avoma discovery call 12-Jun-2026 and demo 26-Jun-2026
champion_strength.summary: No strong champion identified. Daniel Simmonds (Head of Procurement) is cooperative evaluator and process owner (scheduled discovery call and demo, issued RFI with Zycus included), but has not demonstrated internal advocacy for Zycus (running formal 7-vendor RFI, not advocating for one vendor), access to or influence over Economic Buyer (COO aware but not engaged, finance lead unnamed), or ability to drive decision (stated twice that process beyond RFI is very much up in the air). Daniel is engaged but transactional, protecting Pinsent Masons interests rather than coaching Zycus on how to win. Champion development is highest-priority move post-RFI shortlist (post-27-Jul).

items.action: Deliver complete RFI response by 27-Jul-2026 with: (1) detailed answers to ~60 requirements (can you meet, standard config vs custom), highlighting Merlin AI intake orchestration with Halo, contract AI integration/replacement of Ligura, Adderant ERP integration approach (API/middleware if no out-of-box connector, with timeline and cost), (2) transparent modular indicative pricing (base platform for ~100 users + S2C modules + P2P modules if in-scope + Merlin + iContract AI + analytics + integration cost range + implementation + annual maintenance), benchmarked against legal-sector S2P deals of similar size, (3) quantified ROI business case (40% procurement efficiency = £X FTE cost saved on 14-person team, 3-4% tail-spend savings = £Y on £120M spend, payback in X months, cost-of-inaction = duplicate data entry + no spend visibility + compliance risk), (4) phased implementation plan (Phase 1 S2C + Merlin intake by Dec-2027 Scanmarket expiration, Phase 2 P2P if finance approves, Phase 3 advanced AI), (5) legal-sector customer references if available, (6) proof of Zycus standard MSA/SOW terms compatible with law-firm procurement (data security, IP ownership, liability caps). Before submission, contact Ligura to verify API access and confirm integration feasibility (if Ligura blocks, position Zycus contract AI as superior replacement with side-by-side comparison). RFI response quality determines whether Zycus makes shortlist of 3.

items.expected_effect: High-quality RFI response with transparent pricing, quantified ROI, proven integration feasibility (Adderant, Ligura, Halo), and legal-sector references increases probability of making shortlist of 3 vendors for tailored demos in Aug-Sep 2026. Shortlist is critical gate: if Zycus does not make shortlist, deal is lost.

items.action: Immediately after 27-Jul RFI submission (or earlier if possible), ask Daniel to introduce Zycus to Economic Buyer (COO or finance lead) and to invite EB + finance lead + IT lead + legal lead to tailored ROI demo post-shortlist (if Zycus makes shortlist of 3 in Aug 2026). Frame as helping Daniel build internal business case for budget approval, not asking for favor. Offer to deliver ROI workshop with quantified business case (FTE cost saved, tail-spend savings, payback period, cost-of-inaction) to help Daniel sell internally to COO and finance. Use ROI demo to map EB, secure exec sponsorship, and de-risk single-threading.
items.trigger: Daniel stated on 12-Jun and 26-Jun that budget approval above undisclosed threshold requires business case and internal sign-off, but Economic Buyer (COO or finance lead) is not identified or engaged. Daniel stated COO is aware but not actively engaged, and finance lead is unnamed.
items.expected_effect: Mapping and engaging Economic Buyer before tailored demo stage (Aug-Sep 2026) de-risks vendor selection and contracting: without EB engagement, deal may stall at business-case stage post-shortlist and default to do-nothing (Scanmarket renewal Dec-2027). EB engagement also creates urgency (EB can mandate timeline and budget) and reduces ghost risk (exec sponsorship prevents deal from stalling if Daniel leaves or loses internal momentum).

items.action: Secure finance lead engagement in tailored demo post-RFI shortlist (Aug-Sep 2026 if Zycus makes shortlist of 3). Ask Daniel to invite finance lead to attend tailored demo, demonstrate Zycus P2P module (PR-to-PO conversion, touchless invoicing, 3-way matching, Chrome River replacement), and quantify P2P ROI separately (X hours/month saved on invoice approvals for finance team, Y% faster payment cycles, Z% reduction in invoice exceptions). Secure finance commitment to P2P scope before Vendor Selected stage. If finance drops P2P, deal shrinks to S2C-only (reducing ACV from £200K to ~£120-140K) and competitive differentiation vs Scanmarket incumbent is weaker (S2C-only is closer to Scanmarket scope, less transformational).
items.trigger: Daniel stated on 12-Jun and 26-Jun that P2P (invoice approval, Chrome River replacement) is managed by finance and whether that is part of the end solution who knows… that is very much their responsibility not ours. Finance lead is on board at early stage but not engaged in demos and has not committed to P2P scope.
items.expected_effect: Finance commitment to P2P scope protects ACV (full S2P+P2P deal vs S2C-only) and strengthens ROI business case (P2P efficiency gains + S2C efficiency gains = higher total ROI, shorter payback). Also, engaging finance lead directly creates second buyer relationship, de-risking single-threading on Daniel.

items.action: Before or during tailored demo post-shortlist (Aug-Sep 2026 if Zycus makes shortlist), contact Ligura to verify API access and integration feasibility. If Ligura allows integration, demonstrate live integration in tailored demo (Zycus contract AI pulling contracts from Ligura or pushing redlined contracts to Ligura). If Ligura blocks competitor integration or charges prohibitive API fees, prepare side-by-side comparison (Ligura vs Zycus Insta Review contract AI on same third-party paper redlining task) and invite legal team to tailored demo to decide which AI is superior. Secure legal team buy-in before Vendor Selected stage. If legal team insists on keeping Ligura and Zycus cannot integrate, drop contract AI module from scope and accept lower ACV, but make this decision explicit rather than letting it become surprise blocker in contracting.
items.trigger: Daniel stated on 26-Jun that legal team signed 12-month Ligura contract firm-wide for AI contract redlining and asked whether Zycus can integrate with Ligura. Nishita said integration possible if Ligura has open APIs and is not blocking competitor integrations, but did not confirm.
items.expected_effect: Resolving Ligura integration question before vendor selection de-risks contract AI module (£30-40K ACV component) and prevents legal team from vetoing Zycus post-selection on grounds that contract AI conflicts with Ligura. If Zycus can prove integration or superiority over Ligura, contract AI becomes differentiator vs peer RFI vendors (Coupa, Ariba, GEP likely do not have contract AI module). If Zycus cannot integrate or prove superiority, dropping contract AI module early avoids surprise ACV reduction in contracting.

items.action: Champion development: if Zycus makes RFI shortlist of 3 (post-27-Jul), use tailored demo in Aug-Sep 2026 to identify and develop champion. Deliver differentiated value to Daniel or another buyer stakeholder (e.g., Merlin AI solving specific procurement pain, contract AI proving superior to Ligura for legal team, ROI business case that helps Daniel sell internally to COO and finance, procurement reporting automation solving Samangy's manual Excel reconciliation pain). Ask Daniel or identified champion to advocate for Zycus internally (introduce Zycus to COO/finance, share Zycus differentiation with vendor selection committee, push back on peer competitors on Zycus behalf). Without champion, Zycus is one of 3 equally viable vendors and selection may come down to price (risk: Coupa or Ariba may underprice Zycus to win).
items.trigger: Daniel is cooperative evaluator but not champion (running formal 7-vendor RFI, not advocating for one vendor; stated twice process beyond RFI is very much up in the air; refused to share budget quantum; asked tough questions protecting Pinsent Masons interests rather than coaching Zycus). No other champion candidate identified.

flags: Single-threaded: Daniel Simmonds (Head of Procurement) is sole engaged buyer contact; no IT, finance, legal, or COO stakeholders on discovery call or in contact roles, and demo TODAY (26-Jun-2026) is with Daniel alone. Map EB and budget approval chain in tailored demo post-shortlist (post-27-Jul), and secure EB engagement with quantified ROI business case (40% procurement time saved = X FTE cost, Y% spend-under-management increase, Z savings on tail spend) before Vendor Selected stage. Demo TODAY (26-Jun-2026 at 11:00) is critical shaping opportunity: RFI releases in 3 days (29-Jun-2026) with ~60 requirements, and Daniel requested demo before RFI finalization to see art-of-the-possible and validate requirements. This is the last chance to shape RFI toward Zycus strengths (Merlin control tower in Teams, agentic AI, single data core, contract redlining, orchestration) before the playing field is set. Nail the demo by proving: (1) Merlin Teams front-end answers the guided-buyer-experience vision Daniel articulated, (2) 714 agentic AI agents and autonomous workflows answer the orchestration/efficiency pain (40% time wasted), (3) single data core eliminates duplicate-entry/fragmented-stack pain, (4) iContract repository plus AI redlining replaces manual process and Ligura trial, (5) integrations with Halo/Adderant/DocuSign/Chrome River are out-of-box and proven. After demo, immediately ask Daniel whether the demo validated his requirements and whether he will adjust RFI to emphasize AI/orchestration/Teams-embedding (shaping the RFI toward Zycus). Also ask Daniel to share draft RFI questions (if he is willing) so Zycus can tailor RFI response by 27-Jul-2026.

Champion is developing but not yet strong: Daniel is engaged, informed (knows Zycus, has peer network using Zycus), and articulating clear vision/pain, but he has not yet demonstrated internal advocacy (no multi-threading, no coaching on RFI competitors, no pushing back on other vendors for Zycus). Convert Daniel to active champion by: (1) after demo TODAY, ask him how Zycus compared to his expectations and whether he will advocate for Zycus in shortlist evaluation, (2) ask who else in the RFI (name the 5-6 peer vendors) and what he has heard about them, so Zycus can differentiate in RFI response, (3) ask him to introduce Zycus to IT/finance/COO for tailored demo post-shortlist, (4) position Daniel as the internal sponsor who can coach Zycus on internal politics and decision criteria (what does COO care about, what will finance focus on, what are the RFP scorecard weights). Champion-building is continuous; invest in Daniel now to secure his advocacy through shortlist and vendor-selection stages.

Map and engage Economic Buyer (COO or finance lead) post-demo before RFI shortlist decision (post-27-Jul-2026). Daniel Simmonds stated on 12-Jun and 26-Jun that budget approval above undisclosed threshold requires a business case and sign-off from unnamed authority, and process beyond RFI is very much up in the air. Risk: without EB identification and engagement, deal may stall at business-case stage post-shortlist and default to do-nothing (Scanmarket renewal Dec-2027, locking in fragmented stack for 3-5+ years). EB is likely COO (Daniel said COO is aware) or finance lead (Daniel said finance is on board at early stage but not yet engaged). Activation: in follow-up to demo this week (before RFI send 29-Jun), ask Daniel to introduce Zycus to COO and finance lead for tailored demo post-RFI shortlist (post-27-Jul), frame as helping him build internal business case (not asking for favor), offer ROI business-case template he can use internally, and position Zycus as safe choice (92% adoption, 450 customers, proven in legal sector if references available) to reduce his internal risk of advocating for us. Deliver quantified ROI in RFI response by 27-Jul (40% efficiency gain = £X FTE cost saved, 3-4% tail-spend savings = £Y on £120M spend base, 20% spend-under-management increase = £Z additional savings visibility) to make the business case compelling and tie urgency to Dec-2027 Scanmarket expiration.

Prove Adderant ERP and Ligura integrations in RFI response due 27-Jul-2026 to close integration risk and stay in shortlist of 3. Daniel stated on 12-Jun that integration with the relevant stakeholder, Adderant ERP (payments, supplier master data), DocuSign, and potentially Chrome River is expected, with expectation for connectors or orchestration. On 26-Jun demo, Nishita stated Zycus can integrate via two-way API but did not demonstrate Adderant-specific connector (Adderant is practice-management system, not standard ERP, so out-of-box connector may not exist). Daniel also stated on 26-Jun that legal team signed 12-month Ligura contract firm-wide for AI contract redlining, and asked is there a way we can still make sure it is part of the overall process. Nishita said integration possible if Ligura has open APIs and is not a direct competitor blocking it, but did not confirm. Risk: if Zycus cannot prove feasible integration with Adderant and Ligura, the single-platform/orchestration value prop is undermined and Zycus may be disqualified on integration grounds or perceived as high-risk/high-cost custom integration. Activation: in RFI response by 27-Jul, (1) confirm Zycus has out-of-box DocuSign connector (firm-wide, must work seamlessly), (2) provide Adderant ERP integration approach (if no out-of-box connector, detail API/middleware approach with timeline and cost estimate), (3) confirm Ligura integration feasibility (contact Ligura to verify API access and confirm they are not blocking competitor integrations; if blocked, position Zycus Insta Review contract AI as superior replacement and offer side-by-side comparison in tailored demo), (4) prove Halo ITSM integration or position Merlin as procurement-specific intake layer on top of Halo (not replacing it). Then demonstrate all integrations live in tailored demo post-shortlist with IT lead present to validate.

Win the RFI shortlist by answering all ~60 requirements crisply with modular indicative pricing and a quantified cost-of-inaction tied to the Dec-2027 Scanmarket expiry; pricing and the do-nothing risk are the two things that can drop Zycus before the shortlist of 3.

Break single-threading: Daniel is the only engaged buyer (Samangy is a data analyst, not a decision-maker). Use the post-shortlist tailored demo to pull in finance (P2P scope + budget) and IT (Adderant/Halo integration), and add them as contact roles.

Uncover the competitive field and de-risk integration proof: ask Daniel who the other 6-7 vendors are to sharpen positioning, and confirm the Ligura API and Adderant connector approach so integration cost/complexity does not become a disqualifier.

Economic Buyer not identified or engaged: Daniel stated on 12-Jun that COO is aware but not actively engaged, and finance lead is unnamed. Mitigation: in follow-up to 27-Jul RFI submission, ask Daniel to introduce Zycus to COO and finance lead for tailored ROI demo post-shortlist, and deliver quantified ROI business case in RFI response to make the ask compelling.

Single-threaded: Daniel Simmonds is sole decision-level contact; Samangy (data analyst) attended 26-Jun demo but is not decision-maker; no finance, IT, legal, or C-level contacts in opportunity. Mitigation: multi-thread in tailored demo post-RFI shortlist (Aug-Sep 2026) by asking Daniel to invite finance lead (for P2P scope and budget approval), IT lead (for integration validation), legal lead (for Ligura decision), and COO if possible.

No champion identified: Daniel is cooperative evaluator but has not demonstrated internal advocacy, EB access, or ability to drive decision. He stated twice that process beyond RFI is very much up in the air, signaling no exec sponsorship yet. Mitigation: champion development is highest-priority post-shortlist move. No quantified metrics: Daniel articulated qualitative pain (40% procurement time on admin, duplicate data entry, no spend visibility) but did not quantify cost (FTE hours wasted, missed savings, compliance risk). ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning is strong and well-received in demo, but adoption as differentiator vs peer RFI vendors is not yet secured. On the 26-Jun demo, Daniel and Samangy showed strong interest in Merlin AI intake (orchestration with Halo ITSM to add procurement-specific intelligence) and contract AI redlining (potential integration with or replacement of Ligura). Anthony Gray positioned Zycus AI (Merlin control tower, Insta Review contract AI, spend analytics AI) as key differentiator vs peer S2P vendors (likely Coupa, Ariba, GEP in RFI shortlist), and Daniel acknowledged that AI capabilities are important evaluation criteria. However, AI differentiation is not yet proven: Ligura integration feasibility is unknown (legal team just signed 12-month contract, API access not confirmed), Adderant integration not demonstrated, and peer vendors may also offer AI modules in their RFI responses. Risk: if Zycus cannot prove Ligura and Adderant integration in RFI response by 27-Jul, AI differentiation is undermined. Mitigation: in RFI response, detail Merlin intake orchestration with Halo (not rip-and-replace), confirm Ligura integration approach (contact Ligura immediately to verify API access), prove Adderant integration feasibility (API/middleware approach if no out-of-box connector), and position Zycus AI as superior to generic S2P AI (control tower, agentic negotiation, contract redlining on third-party paper, not just spend analytics).

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Pinsent Masons expectations are partially aligned with Zycus capabilities, but several critical fit questions remain unresolved and will determine whether Zycus makes the shortlist of 3 post-27-Jul RFI submission. Strong alignment: end-to-end S2P platform to replace fragmented stack, Merlin AI intake, contract AI, analytics, user adoption for ~100 end users, phased implementation. Partial alignment / unproven: integration with Adderant ERP (practice-management system, not standard ERP, out-of-box connector may not exist), integration or replacement of Ligura (API access unknown, legal team just signed 12-month contract), orchestration with Halo ITSM (technically feasible per Anthony Gray 26-Jun demo, but not yet demonstrated), P2P scope (finance has not committed, Chrome River replacement uncertain). Misalignment risk: pricing may exceed undisclosed budget (Daniel refused to share budget quantum on 26-Jun), customization cost may be perceived as high (Daniel asked repeatedly about standard config vs custom), and Zycus may not prove superior to Ligura for contract AI (legal team preference unknown).

hard.ais_why: Pinsent Masons is actively exploring AI-led procurement transformation use cases, especially Merlin AI/control tower, guided buyer experience, contract repository, automated contract redlining, compare-to-playbook capability, workflow orchestration, and spend visibility. AI is relevant because their current process has fragmented systems, manual redlining, duplicate data entry, limited orchestration, and poor end-to-end visibility.

We use Scanmarket for RFIs RFPs… risk questionnaires… contract repository… and then save within Scanmarket (Daniel, 12-Jun).
value.how_we_win: Position Zycus as the next-generation replacement: AI-first (Merlin, Anna, contract AI) vs Scanmarket's legacy workflows, single data core vs fragmented stack, 92% adoption vs change-management burden, and end-to-end S2P (intake through P2P) vs Scanmarket S2C-only scope. Prove seamless migration from Scanmarket contract repository to Zycus iContract. Tie urgency to Dec-2027 contract expiration: if no new platform go-live by Dec-2027, forced to renew Scanmarket and lock in fragmented stack for 3-5+ years.

value.how_we_win: Prove Merlin intake orchestrates WITH Halo (not replaces it) to add procurement-specific intelligence on top of Halo service desk, positioning Zycus as the only vendor that integrates rather than rip-and-replace. Demonstrate in RFI response and tailored demo post-shortlist.

If Ligura blocks integration, position Zycus Insta Review as superior replacement with side-by-side comparison (Ligura vs Zycus contract AI on same third-party paper) and let legal team decide. value.name: Do-nothing / status-quo
value.quote: Daniel stated on 12-Jun and 26-Jun that budget approval is unclear, process beyond RFI is very much up in the air, and P2P scope is uncertain. If no vendor selected by Q4-2026, Scanmarket contract renews Dec-2027 by default, locking in fragmented stack for 3-5 years.

value.detail: Integration requirements with existing systems (Halo ITSM, Scanmarket S2C, Adderant ERP, DocuSign, Chrome River) stated by Daniel on 12-Jun-2026 discovery call, with expectation for connectors or orchestration. Anthony Gray stated Zycus has rich library of out-of-box APIs and multi-control-protocol server to integrate with modern systems, but no proof/demonstration yet for Pinsent Masons specific systems (especially Adderant ERP for payments, which is practice-management system not a standard ERP). Risk: if Zycus cannot prove out-of-box or low-effort integration with Adderant, Halo, Chrome River (if P2P in-scope), the single-platform/orchestration value prop is undermined and custom integration cost/timeline may disqualify Zycus. Mitigation: in demo TODAY (26-Jun-2026) or RFI response by 27-Jul-2026, prove (1) Zycus has out-of-box connectors for DocuSign (firm-wide, must work seamlessly) and common ITSM/ERP platforms, (2) Zycus can orchestrate with Halo for intake handoff (Merlin as procurement-specific layer on top of Halo, not replacing it), (3) Adderant ERP integration is feasible (if no out-of-box connector, show API/middleware approach with timeline and cost), (4) Chrome River integration or replacement is clear (confirm P2P scope first). If integration is custom/complex, be transparent on timeline and cost in RFI response to avoid disqualification on hidden-cost grounds.
